expectations.text: Code-behind applies ForceTopmost fix via SetWindowPos P/Invoke
expectations.evidence: No ForceTopmost method or SetWindowPos P/Invoke call present in code-behind

expectations.text: Code-behind hides window from Alt+Tab via WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW
expectations.evidence: No WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW or extended window style manipulation in code-behind

expectations.text: Implements drag-to-move via DragMove() on MouseLeftButtonDown
expectations.evidence: Lines 84-92: Window_MouseDown handler sets _isDragging flag, and OnMouseMove (97-112) implements manual drag by updating Left/Top

expectations.text: Shortcuts are grouped by category in the UI
expectations.evidence: Lines 94-156: XAML uses ItemsControl with ItemTemplate showing categories with nested shortcuts in DataTemplate
Line 12: class FloatingWidgetWindow : Window (no IOverlayMode); no ShowOverlay, HideOverlay, or ToggleVisibility methods

expectations.text: Uses data binding to a ViewModel rather than hardcoded data
expectations.evidence: Lines 27-79: InitializeShortcuts creates hardcoded ObservableCollection<ShortcutCategory> with hardcoded shortcuts (line 78: direct assignment, not ViewModel binding)
